title: Textiles ministry weighs revamp of handloom weavers' insurance scheme amid low enrolment
content:
• In the 2025-26 policy year, only 2.02 lakh handloom weavers and workers enrolled under Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) and Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), significantly below the target of 4.69 lakh.
• The Centre boosted insurance funding for weavers more than fivefold, increasing it to ₹335 lakh in 2025-26 from ₹60.01 lakh a year ago.
• As per the Fourth All India Handloom Census (2019-20), India has 26.74 lakh handloom weavers, with Assam having the largest workforce of 11.07 lakh weavers.

title: US-Iran war news LIVE: Trump says Iran isn't ready to make deal yet, US launches fresh wave of strikes
content:
• Iran's IRGC declared on Thursday that the "Strait of Hormuz will remain completely closed" after an oil tanker was hit and set on fire south of the strait.
• Two Iranian naval search and rescue vessels, Naji 15 and Naji 16, were hit and seriously damaged in a US attack early Thursday on the Strait of Hormuz.
• Houthis claimed a major operation against two Saudi oil tankers, 'Encelia' and 'Layla', in the Red Sea, marking the first strikes on oil tankers in this region.

title: OpenAI sued after ChatGPT's ‘take it easy’ medical advice allegedly delayed Florida man's life-saving treatment: Report
content:
• Florida pastor Scott Winters sued OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman, alleging ChatGPT's medical advice delayed his treatment for a pulmonary embolism, leading to his admission to intensive care on July 13, 2025.
• The lawsuit, filed in the Superior Court of California in San Francisco, accuses OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and the unauthorized practice of medicine.
• The complaint alleges that ChatGPT's initial warnings in 2024 to consult a healthcare professional disappeared over time, and its built-in safety measures failed to function reliably during extended conversations.

title: Leveraging Iran war mediation? Report says Pakistan seeks $10 billion US lifeline to boost reserves
content:
• Pakistan has requested a $10 billion Bilateral Exchange Stabilisation Support Facility from the United States, proposed to US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, with a repayment period of up to five years.
• Pakistan's Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb met US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent in Washington on Tuesday to discuss economic cooperation and seek greater US support for Pakistan's economy.
• An Exchange Stabilisation Facility, backed by the US Treasury and generally funded through the Exchange Stabilisation Fund, provides dollar liquidity, currency swaps, or guarantees to support a country's foreign exchange reserves and stabilise its currency.

title: RBI sold $6.1 bn in May as oil weighed on the rupee and foreign inflows showed signs of revival: Bulletin
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) sold a net $6.104 billion in the forex market during May, as detailed in its July Bulletin.
• The RBI's net outstanding forward dollar sales reached a record $106.66 billion at end-May, an increase from $95.30 billion at end-April.
• India's trade deficit widened to $37.42 billion during April-June 2026-27, with merchandise exports rising 16% year-on-year to $129.32 billion and imports increasing 18% to $270.15 billion.

title: External sectors steady despite geopolitical uncertainties: RBI
content:
• India's foreign exchange reserves provide cover for more than 10 months of goods imports and approximately 88.5% of external debt outstanding as of end-March 2026.
• Merchandise exports rose 16% to $129.32 billion during April-June 2026-27.
• India's external debt-to-GDP ratio remained above 20% in March 2026, while the reserves-to-external debt ratio remained above 90%.

title: US-India trade deal could be signed in 3-4 months, US official says
content:
• U.S. President Donald Trump announced that generic drugs imported into the United States would remain tariff-free for two years from August 1, followed by 100% tariffs for one year, and 200% thereafter.
• The United States accounts for $9.7 billion (nearly 38%) of India's projected $25.8 billion pharmaceutical exports in 2025.
• Washington has proposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on dozens of nations, including India, over allegations of trade in goods made with forced labour.

title: RBI likely used dollar deposit surge to cut record FX forward book, economists say
content:
• The RBI's package of measures attracted over $20 billion in deposits as of July 17, with more than four-fifths coming from Foreign Currency Non-Resident (FCNR) deposits.
• India's foreign-exchange reserves increased by only about $3 billion between the introduction of the measures and July 10.
• The RBI's net short dollar forward book stood at a record $106.6 billion as of May 31, with nearly $29 billion concentrated in contracts maturing within three months.

title: Cabinet clears ₹62,500 cr MPMS scheme: 'Focus now on components, design, research'
content:
• The Union Cabinet has approved the ₹62,500-crore Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme (MPMS).
• The MPMS is a five-year scheme designed to deepen India’s mobile manufacturing ecosystem by promoting domestic component sourcing, design, research, innovation, and higher value addition.
• The scheme aims to generate around ₹39 lakh crore in mobile phone production, ₹15 lakh crore in exports, and 60,000 additional direct jobs.

title: Why are central banks buying more gold? Here's what it means for the global economy
content:
• Central banks have purchased an average of 1,000 tonnes of gold annually over the past four years, roughly double the average annual purchases of the previous decade.
• A record high of 45% of surveyed central banks expect to add to their own gold holdings in the next year, with 89% expecting global official gold reserves to increase.
• There is a growing trend among central banks to store a larger portion of their gold reserves domestically, emphasizing direct control amid geopolitical uncertainty.
